article_title: CMS and FDA Launch RAPID Coverage Pathway, Compressing Medicare Reimbursement for Breakthrough Devices From a Year to Two Months

content_preview.title: CMS and FDA Launch RAPID Coverage Pathway, Compressing Medicare Reimbursement for Breakthrough Devices From a Year to Two Months
content_preview.summary: A new joint program from FDA and CMS would issue a proposed Medicare coverage decision the same day a breakthrough device wins FDA authorization, aiming to cut total time-to-coverage from roughly a year or more to about two months.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The U.S. Food and Drug Administration and the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services on April 23 announced a joint coverage pathway designed to close the reimbursement gap that has historically left Medicare patients waiting more than a year after a breakthrough medical device clears the FDA. According to the [FDA's press announcement](https://www.fda.gov/news-events/press-announcements/cms-and-fda-announce-rapid-coverage-pathway-accelerate-patient-access-life-changing-medical-dev...

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong Policy & Regulation / Biotech & Medicine News piece. All 3 prior findings cleanly addressed.
editor_notes.source_verification: Re-verified. Quotes (Makary 'functioning as a single team', Oz 'each play a critical role / aligned sooner', Oz 'brings our two agencies together earlier') now repointed to FDA source-0 where they appear verbatim. Bloomberg-only specifics ('40 devices', '20 more', technology categories, Medtronic/Boston Scientific/Abbott manufacturer names) all removed. Bloomberg URL dropped from sources entirely (3→2 sources). Article relies on FDA + STAT, both readable and authoritative.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Clean APPROVE on re-review. Ready to merge.
